title: Bump github.com/containers/image/v5 from 5.2.1 to 5.3.1
body:
Bumps [github.com/containers/image/v5](https://github.com/containers/image) from 5.2.1 to 5.3.1.
Release notes

Sourced from github.com/containers/image/v5's releases.

v5.3.0

  • Fixed registries.conf
  • Add Code of Conduct
  • Bubbling up errors returned by copyBlobFromStream
  • Re-order the members of archiveReference to a more logical order
  • Add docker/archive.NewReference
  • Remove tarballReference.transport
  • Add tarball.NewReference
  • Add storage.Transport.GetStoreIfSet
  • Simplify ID value check
  • Add validateImageID
  • Add storage.Transport.NewStoreReference
  • registries.conf.d: add stances for the registries.conf
  • Add $HOME/.config/containers/registries.conf to config path
  • sysregistriesv2: drop "rewrite" debug messages
  • Add $HOME/.config/containers/certs.d to perHostCertDirPath
  • Intial authfile man page
  • Return error when acquire semaphore lock gets error
  • feat(docker): support docker registry token
  • containers-registries.conf.d: refine semantics
  • Updated warning about short names.
  • Make UpdatedImage update after conversion of MIME type
  • Support IdentityToken in registry authn
  • Deprecate SupportsEncryption call in manifest interface
  • oci manifest: do not require whitelist of layer types
  • Fix crash on missing RootFS in docker/tarfile configs
  • copy: try list conversions on list push failures
  • Support architecture variants for multi-arch images and runtime compatibility checks
Commits
  • 4276a4f v5.3.0
  • 023117c Merge pull request #854 from mtrmac/arch-variant
  • 812733e Support architecture variants for multi-arch images and runtime compatibility...
  • 2e70342 Merge pull request #820 from nalind/manifest-list-retry-using-conversions
  • 062db46 copy: try list conversions on list push failures
  • d8b77ec Merge pull request #853 from mtrmac/docker-tarfile-crash
  • 9ff92e0 Fix crash on missing RootFS in docker/tarfile configs
  • 75243b0 Update containers/storage to v1.16.5
  • cd2fbca oci manifest: do not require whitelist of layer types
  • d66f61b Deprecate SupportsEncryption call in manifest interface
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

comments.body: So will buildah work with that token? How does buildah create the token?  Should we make buildah more aware of the token?

As long as Buildah does _no changes_ to the configuration, it should automatically use the token.

Obtaining the token is not part of the c/image PR. In some cases it might be possible to get it using an username/password pair, but that’s not currently implemented (and we would have to somehow decide whether to use the old flow or the OAuth2 one).

In the Amazon case, there is apparently a specialized utility to obtain and write the token (per https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/533#issuecomment-511207242 ). I don’t think it’s particularly useful for Buildah / Podman to duplicate that functionality, especially if it is specific to that registry implementation; at most I would hope for is a warning from `buildah login` saying “you have an identity token configured; do you really want to authenticate using an user name+ password pair?”.
@mtrmac I am now throwing an error if  you have logged in and have the identity token when attempting  a login.

> @mtrmac I am now throwing an error if you have logged in and have the identity token when attempting a login.

I can’t see that here (yet). I did notice that code in the Podman update.

Oops pushed to wrong place it is there now.

https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2241/commits/54c40b31e236e0189b25897ab92d6231ba38a59b#diff-90bc643dbf475164da9769a1eccf8583R79
@mtrmac Have you found what the issue was for containers/image?
I know what’s the problem, I’m about 50% done with a fix.

> I know what’s the problem, I’m about 50% done with a fix.

https://github.com/mtrmac/image/tree/updates-s2s1 if anyone wanted to test this _now_; it’s working but not yet ready for merging, and I suppose we’ll want a 5.3.1 release.
Can you open a PR with Podman to see if it passes the tests?

A newer version of github.com/containers/image/v5 exists, but since this PR has been edited by someone other than Dependabot I haven't updated it. You'll get a PR for the updated version as normal once this PR is merged.
This should have the same `login` changes that were made to Podman.
Are you indicating that it does not?
Oh, my mistake, I had the file marked as “viewed” and didn’t notice it. I’m sorry.
